html:
[1246]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 公廉得情 - Means 'the official, through his integrity, grasped the truth.' Here, '公廉' refers to the official's integrity and fairness.
  • 以周抵罪 - Means 'had Zhou bear the blame.' This indicates that Zhou was made to take responsibility for the crime.
  • 共服其神 - Means 'all admired his skill.' This suggests that everyone was impressed by the official's ability to solve the case.
  • 而不知所以能察之故 - Means 'but did not know the reason for his ability to discern it.' This indicates that the official's method of solving the case was not understood by others.
  • 事无难辨 - Means 'there is nothing difficult to discern.' This is a general statement about the nature of discernment.
  • 要在随处留心耳 - Means 'it lies in paying attention everywhere.' This emphasizes the importance of being observant.
  • 初验尸时 - Means 'when first examining the body.' This refers to the initial stage of the investigation.
  • 见银袱刺万字文 - Means 'saw the silver cloth embroidered with a 'man' character.' This describes a specific detail observed during the examination.
  • 周袱亦然 - Means 'Zhou's cloth was the same.' This indicates that the cloth found with Zhou had the same embroidery.
  • 诘之 - Means 'questioned him.' This refers to the act of interrogating Zhou.
  • 又云无旧 - Means 'he said he had no prior acquaintance.' This indicates that Zhou denied any previous connection to the victim.
  • 词貌诡变 - Means 'his words and demeanor were deceitful.' This suggests that Zhou's behavior was suspicious.
  • 是以确知其真凶也 - Means 'therefore, it was confirmed that he was the real culprit.' This concludes the official's reasoning.
  • 异史氏曰 - Means 'The historian of strange tales said.' This introduces a commentary or reflection.
  • 世之折狱者 - Means 'those who adjudicate cases in the world.' This refers to judges or officials who handle legal cases.
  • 非悠悠置之 - Means 'do not dismiss them carelessly.' This suggests that cases should be taken seriously.
  • 缧系数十人而狼藉之耳 - Means 'they tie up dozens of people and treat them indiscriminately.' This criticizes the practice of arresting many people without proper evidence.
  • 堂上肉鼓吹 - Means 'the sound of drums and music in the courtroom.' This describes a chaotic and improper atmosphere in the courtroom.
  • 喧阗旁午 - Means 'noisy and chaotic.' This emphasizes the disorder in the courtroom.
  • 颦蹙曰 - Means 'frowned and said.' This describes the judge's expression and words.
  • 我劳心民事也 - Means 'I am working hard for the people's affairs.' This is a self-justification by the judge.
  • 云板三敲 - Means 'the gong is struck three times.' This refers to the signal for the judge to begin the hearing.
  • 声色并进 - Means 'sound and color advance together.' This describes a chaotic and improper atmosphere.
  • 难决之词 - Means 'difficult-to-decide words.' This refers to complex or ambiguous statements.
  • 不复置念 - Means 'no longer consider it.' This suggests that the judge has given up on solving the case.
  • 专待升堂时 - Means 'only wait for the time to ascend the hall.' This indicates that the judge is waiting for the formal hearing.
  • 祸桑树以烹老龟耳 - Means 'to bring disaster to the mulberry tree and boil the old turtle.' This is a metaphor for unjust punishment.
  • 民情何由得哉 - Means 'how can the people's feelings be understood?' This criticizes the inability of officials to understand the people.
  • 智者不必仁 - Means 'the wise do not necessarily have to be benevolent.' This distinguishes between wisdom and benevolence.
  • 而仁者则必智 - Means 'but the benevolent must be wise.' This suggests that benevolence requires wisdom.
  • 盖用心苦则机关出也 - Means 'because those who work hard will find the mechanism.' This emphasizes the importance of diligence.
  • 随在留心 - Means 'pay attention everywhere.' This reiterates the importance of being observant.
  • 可以教天下之宰民社者矣 - Means 'this can teach those who govern the people and the state.' This concludes the historian's reflection.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 公廉得情 - Refers to the official's integrity and fairness in solving the case.
  • 以周抵罪 - Refers to the practice of making someone take responsibility for a crime.
  • 共服其神 - Refers to the admiration of the official's skill in solving the case.
  • 而不知所以能察之故 - Refers to the mystery of the official's method of solving the case.
  • 事无难辨 - Refers to the general principle that nothing is too difficult to discern.
  • 要在随处留心耳 - Refers to the importance of being observant in all situations.
  • 初验尸时 - Refers to the initial stage of a criminal investigation.
  • 见银袱刺万字文 - Refers to a specific detail observed during the examination of the body.
  • 周袱亦然 - Refers to the similarity between the cloth found with the victim and the one found with Zhou.
  • 诘之 - Refers to the act of interrogating a suspect.
  • 又云无旧 - Refers to the suspect's denial of any prior acquaintance with the victim.
  • 词貌诡变 - Refers to the suspect's suspicious behavior.
  • 是以确知其真凶也 - Refers to the conclusion that the suspect is the real culprit.
  • 异史氏曰 - Refers to the historian of strange tales, who provides a commentary on the case.
  • 世之折狱者 - Refers to judges or officials who handle legal cases.
  • 非悠悠置之 - Refers to the criticism of dismissing cases carelessly.
  • 缧系数十人而狼藉之耳 - Refers to the criticism of arresting many people without proper evidence.
  • 堂上肉鼓吹 - Refers to the chaotic atmosphere in the courtroom.
  • 喧阗旁午 - Refers to the noisy and chaotic environment in the courtroom.
  • 颦蹙曰 - Refers to the judge's expression and words.
  • 我劳心民事也 - Refers to the judge's self-justification.
  • 云板三敲 - Refers to the signal for the judge to begin the hearing.
  • 声色并进 - Refers to the chaotic atmosphere in the courtroom.
  • 难决之词 - Refers to complex or ambiguous statements.
  • 不复置念 - Refers to the judge's giving up on solving the case.
  • 专待升堂时 - Refers to the judge's waiting for the formal hearing.
  • 祸桑树以烹老龟耳 - Refers to the metaphor of unjust punishment.
  • 民情何由得哉 - Refers to the criticism of the inability of officials to understand the people.
  • 智者不必仁 - Refers to the distinction between wisdom and benevolence.
  • 而仁者则必智 - Refers to the idea that benevolence requires wisdom.
  • 盖用心苦则机关出也 - Refers to the importance of diligence in finding solutions.
  • 随在留心 - Refers to the importance of being observant.
  • 可以教天下之宰民社者矣 - Refers to the historian's reflection on the importance of being observant for those who govern.

[1247]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 世有隙 - Long-standing feud or conflict.
  • 屈意交欢 - To lower oneself to seek friendship or reconciliation.
  • 实相告 - To tell the truth.
  • 确有证据 - Certainly has evidence.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 邑 - Refers to a town or village, a common administrative unit in ancient China.
  • 南山 - Refers to South Mountain, a specific location mentioned in the text.
  • 眢井 - Refers to a dry well, a common feature in rural areas.
  • 郑伦 - A proper name, likely a significant character in the story.
  • 产主 - Refers to the owner of the property, a term used in legal and social contexts.

Proper Nouns

  • 胡成 - The main character of the story.
  • 冯安 - Another character in the story, with whom Tru Cheng has a feud.
  • 郑伦 - A significant character, possibly involved in the property transaction.
  • 南山 - The location of the dry well.
  • 邑 - The town or village where the story takes place.

[1248]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 逾日 - Temporal phrase meaning 'after a day' or 'the next day.'
  • 自言为亡者妻 - Phrase indicating that the woman claims to be the wife of the deceased.
  • 夫何甲 - Formal way of addressing the deceased husband, meaning 'my husband, named Jia.'
  • 揭数百金作贸易 - Phrase describing the deceased's activity, meaning 'carried hundreds of gold coins for trade.'
  • 被胡杀死 - Phrase indicating that the deceased was killed by someone named 'Hu.'
  • 井有死人 - Phrase meaning 'there is a dead person in the well.'
  • 恐未必即是汝夫 - Phrase expressing doubt, meaning 'it is not necessarily your husband.'
  • 执言甚坚 - Phrase meaning 'insists very firmly.'
  • 视之果不妄 - Phrase meaning 'upon inspection, it is indeed not mistaken.'
  • 不敢近 - Phrase meaning 'dare not approach.'
  • 却立而号 - Phrase meaning 'stands back and cries.'
  • 真犯已得 - Phrase meaning 'the true culprit has been apprehended.'
  • 骸躯未全 - Phrase meaning 'the body is incomplete.'
  • 暂归 - Phrase meaning 'go back temporarily.'
  • 得死者首 - Phrase meaning 'get the head of the deceased.'
  • 招报令其抵偿 - Phrase meaning 'report to have him compensate.'
  • 自狱中唤胡出 - Phrase meaning 'called Hu out from the prison.'
  • 呵曰 - Phrase meaning 'said sternly.'
  • 明日不将头至 - Phrase meaning 'if you do not bring the head tomorrow.'
  • 当械折股 - Phrase meaning 'I will have you chained and your limbs broken.'
  • 押去终日而返 - Phrase meaning 'taken away all day and returned.'
  • 诘之 - Phrase meaning 'questioned him.'
  • 但有号泣 - Phrase meaning 'only wept.'
  • 以梏具置前作刑势 - Phrase meaning 'placed shackles in front as if to punish.'
  • 却又不刑 - Phrase meaning 'but did not actually punish.'
  • 当夜扛尸忙迫 - Phrase meaning 'in the night, carrying the body in haste.'
  • 不知坠落何处 - Phrase meaning 'did not know where it fell.'
  • 奈何不细寻之 - Phrase meaning 'why didn't you search carefully?'
  • 哀祈容急觅 - Phrase meaning 'begged for permission to search urgently.'
  • 少妇勿复出入公门 - Phrase meaning 'young wife, do not come to the public office again.'
  • 感泣 - Phrase meaning 'moved to tears.'
  • 叩头而下 - Phrase meaning 'knelt and went down.'
  • 票示里人 - Phrase meaning 'posted a notice to the villagers.'
  • 代觅其首 - Phrase meaning 'to help find the head.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 亡者妻 - Refers to the wife of the deceased, a common role in classical Chinese narratives.
  • 揭数百金作贸易 - Describes a common economic activity in ancient China, where merchants would carry large sums of money for trade.
  • 被胡杀死 - 'Hu' could be a specific person's name or a generic term for a bandit or criminal.
  • 井有死人 - Wells were common in ancient Chinese villages, and finding a dead body in a well was a significant event.
  • 骸躯未全 - This phrase suggests that the body is incomplete, possibly due to decomposition or damage.
  • 招报令其抵偿 - This phrase indicates a legal process where the culprit is required to compensate the victim's family.
  • 械折股 - A severe punishment involving chaining and breaking limbs, reflecting the harshness of ancient Chinese justice.
  • 少妇勿复出入公门 - This phrase reflects the social norms of the time, where women were expected to avoid public offices unless necessary.

Proper Nouns

  • 甲 - The name of the deceased husband.
  • 胡 - The name of the suspect or the bandit who killed the deceased.
  • 公 - A generic term for the official or judge handling the case.

[1249]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 經宿 - Indicates a short period of time, reflecting the urgency and efficiency of the investigation.
  • 報稱已獲 - Reflects the traditional method of reporting and verifying crimes.
  • 問驗既明 - Emphasizes the importance of thorough investigation and verification in Chinese legal procedures.
  • 賞以千錢 - Shows the use of rewards to encourage cooperation and information.
  • 大案已成 - Highlights the significance of solving major cases.
  • 然人命重大 - Reflects the gravity of taking human life in Chinese culture.
  • 非積歲不能成結 - Indicates the complexity and time-consuming nature of resolving serious cases.
  • 少婦亦難存活 - Reflects the social and economic challenges faced by widows in traditional Chinese society.
  • 早令適人 - Suggests the practical advice given to ensure the woman's well-being.
  • 此后亦无他务 - Indicates the conclusion of the case and the end of further responsibilities.
  • 但有上台检驳 - Reflects the hierarchical nature of the legal system and the potential for higher authorities to challenge decisions.
  • 止须汝应声耳 - Shows the limited role of the official in responding to higher authority.
  • 飞两签下 - Refers to the physical punishment used to enforce obedience.
  • 再辩，又一签下 - Indicates the use of physical punishment to suppress dissent.
  • 公极意慰谕之 - Reflects the official's role in providing emotional support and guidance.
  • 有买妇者，当堂关白 - Suggests the importance of transparency and reporting in personal matters.
  • 盖即报人头之王五也 - Reveals the true identity of the informant.
  • 我久知其情 - Indicates the official's long-term knowledge of the case.
  • 所以迟迟而发者 - Explains the delay in taking action.
  • 恐有万一之屈耳 - Reflects the concern for fairness and justice.
  • 尸未出井 - Indicates the physical evidence that was crucial to the case.
  • 何以确信为汝夫 - Questions the certainty of the identification.
  • 盖先知其死矣 - Reveals the prior knowledge of the victim's death.
  • 且甲死犹衣败絮 - Provides a detail about the victim's condition.
  • 数百金何所自来 - Questions the source of the money, highlighting the financial aspect of the case.
  • 头之所在，汝何知之熟也 - Questions the informant's familiarity with the location of the head.
  • 所以如此其急者 - Explains the urgency of the case.
  • 意在速合耳 - Reveals the intention to quickly resolve the case.
  • 并械之 - Indicates the use of physical restraints to control suspects.
  • 果吐其实 - Shows the outcome of the interrogation.
  • 盖王五与妇私已久 - Reveals the underlying relationship between the suspects.
  • 谋杀其夫 - Highlights the crime of conspiracy to murder.
  • 而适值胡成之戏也 - Provides the context of the prank that coincided with the crime.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 经宿 - Indicates a short period of time, reflecting the urgency and efficiency of the investigation.
  • 报称已获 - Reflects the traditional method of reporting and verifying crimes.
  • 问验既明 - Emphasizes the importance of thorough investigation and verification in Chinese legal procedures.
  • 赏以千钱 - Shows the use of rewards to encourage cooperation and information.
  • 大案已成 - Highlights the significance of solving major cases.
  • 然人命重大 - Reflects the gravity of taking human life in Chinese culture.
  • 非积岁不能成结 - Indicates the complexity and time-consuming nature of resolving serious cases.
  • 少妇亦难存活 - Reflects the social and economic challenges faced by widows in traditional Chinese society.
  • 早令适人 - Suggests the practical advice given to ensure the woman's well-being.
  • 此后亦无他务 - Indicates the conclusion of the case and the end of further responsibilities.
  • 但有上台检驳 - Reflects the hierarchical nature of the legal system and the potential for higher authorities to challenge decisions.
  • 止须汝应声耳 - Shows the limited role of the official in responding to higher authority.
  • 飞两签下 - Refers to the physical punishment used to enforce obedience.
  • 再辩，又一签下 - Indicates the use of physical punishment to suppress dissent.
  • 公极意慰谕之 - Reflects the official's role in providing emotional support and guidance.
  • 有买妇者，当堂关白 - Suggests the importance of transparency and reporting in personal matters.
  • 盖即报人头之王五也 - Reveals the true identity of the informant.
  • 我久知其情 - Indicates the official's long-term knowledge of the case.
  • 所以迟迟而发者 - Explains the delay in taking action.
  • 恐有万一之屈耳 - Reflects the concern for fairness and justice.
  • 尸未出井 - Indicates the physical evidence that was crucial to the case.
  • 何以确信为汝夫 - Questions the certainty of the identification.
  • 盖先知其死矣 - Reveals the prior knowledge of the victim's death.
  • 且甲死犹衣败絮 - Provides a detail about the victim's condition.
  • 数百金何所自来 - Questions the source of the money, highlighting the financial aspect of the case.
  • 头之所在，汝何知之熟也 - Questions the informant's familiarity with the location of the head.
  • 所以如此其急者 - Explains the urgency of the case.
  • 意在速合耳 - Reveals the intention to quickly resolve the case.
  • 并械之 - Indicates the use of physical restraints to control suspects.
  • 果吐其实 - Shows the outcome of the interrogation.
  • 盖王五与妇私已久 - Reveals the underlying relationship between the suspects.
  • 谋杀其夫 - Highlights the crime of conspiracy to murder.
  • 而适值胡成之戏也 - Provides the context of the prank that coincided with the crime.

Proper Nouns

  • 王五 - The name of the informant.
  • 甲叔 - The uncle of the victim.

[1250]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 乃释胡 - 乃 indicates a shift in action, meaning 'then' or 'thereupon,' and 释胡 means 'released the Hu people.' This phrase marks a significant action in the narrative.
  • 以诬告重笞 - 以 means 'for' or 'because of,' 诬告 means 'false accusation,' and 重笞 means 'severe flogging.' This phrase describes the punishment for a false accusation.
  • 徒三年 - 徒 means 'imprisonment,' and 三年 means 'three years.' This phrase specifies the duration of the sentence.
  • 事结，并未妄刑一人 - 事结 means 'the matter was resolved,' and 并未妄刑一人 means 'no one was wrongly punished.' This phrase concludes the legal proceedings with a statement of fairness.
  • 异史氏曰 - 异史氏 refers to the narrator or author, and 曰 means 'said.' This phrase introduces a commentary or reflection by the author.
  • 我夫子有仁爱名 - 我夫子 means 'my master' or 'my teacher,' and 有仁爱名 means 'is known for benevolence.' This phrase praises the teacher's reputation.
  • 即此一事，亦以见仁人之用心苦矣 - 即此一事 means 'even this one matter,' 亦以见 means 'also shows,' 仁人之用心 means 'the benevolent person's intentions,' and 苦矣 means 'are arduous.' This phrase reflects on the difficulty of the teacher's benevolent intentions.
  • 方宰淄时 - 方 means 'when,' 宰 means 'governor,' 淄 is the name of a place, and 时 means 'time.' This phrase sets the time and place of the teacher's governance.
  • 松裁弱冠 - 松 is a name, 裁 means 'just,' and 弱冠 means 'in his early twenties.' This phrase describes the narrator's age.
  • 过蒙器许 - 过蒙 means 'graciously received,' and 器许 means 'high praise.' This phrase expresses gratitude for the teacher's recognition.
  • 而驾钝不才 - 而 means 'but,' 驾 means 'ride,' 钝 means 'dull,' and 不才 means 'unworthy.' This phrase describes the narrator's self-perception as unworthy.
  • 竟以不舞之鹤为羊公辱 - 竟 means 'finally,' 以 means 'as,' 不舞之鹤 means 'a non-dancing crane,' 为 means 'by,' 羊公 means 'Mr. Yang,' and 辱 means 'insulted.' This phrase describes the narrator's feeling of being insulted.
  • 是我夫子生平有不哲之一事 - 是 means 'this,' 我夫子 means 'my master,' 生平 means 'throughout his life,' and 有不哲之一事 means 'had one unwise act.' This phrase reflects on the teacher's imperfection.
  • 则松实贻之也 - 则 means 'then,' 松 is the narrator's name, and 实贻之也 means 'indeed caused it.' This phrase attributes the teacher's imperfection to the narrator.
  • 悲夫 - 悲夫 is an expression of sorrow or lamentation. This phrase expresses the narrator's regret.
  • 义犬 - 义犬 means 'loyal dog.' This phrase introduces the theme of loyalty.
  • 周村有贾某贸易芜湖 - 周村 is the name of a village, 有 means 'has,' 贾某 means 'a merchant named Jia,' 贸易 means 'traded,' and 芜湖 is the name of a city. This phrase introduces the merchant and his business.
  • 获重资 - 获 means 'obtained,' and 重资 means 'large sum of money.' This phrase describes the merchant's success.
  • 赁舟将归 - 赁 means 'rented,' 舟 means 'boat,' and 将归 means 'was returning home.' This phrase describes the merchant's journey home.
  • 见堤上有屠人缚犬 - 见 means 'saw,' 堤上 means 'on the dike,' 有 means 'had,' 屠人 means 'butcher,' and 缚犬 means 'tied up a dog.' This phrase describes the merchant's encounter.
  • 倍价赎之 - 倍价 means 'double the price,' and 赎之 means 'redeemed it.' This phrase describes the merchant's action to save the dog.
  • 养豢舟上 - 养豢 means 'kept,' and 舟上 means 'on the boat.' This phrase describes the dog's new home.
  • 舟上固积寇也 - 舟上 means 'on the boat,' 固 means 'indeed,' and 积寇 means 'bandits.' This phrase reveals the true nature of the boat's occupants.
  • 窥客装 - 窥 means 'spied,' and 客装 means 'the guest's belongings.' This phrase describes the bandits' actions.
  • 荡舟入莽 - 荡舟 means 'sailed away,' and 入莽 means 'into the wilderness.' This phrase describes the bandits' escape.
  • 操刀欲杀 - 操刀 means 'took a knife,' and 欲杀 means 'wanted to kill.' This phrase describes the bandits' intent.
  • 贾哀赐以全尸 - 贾 is the merchant's name, 哀 means 'pitied,' 赐以 means 'granted,' and 全尸 means 'a whole body.' This phrase describes the merchant's plea for mercy.
  • 盗乃以毡裹置江中 - 盗 means 'bandits,' 乃 means 'then,' 以毡裹 means 'wrapped in a blanket,' and 置江中 means 'placed in the river.' This phrase describes the bandits' cruel act.
  • 犬见之，哀嗥投水 - 犬 means 'dog,' 见之 means 'saw it,' 哀嗥 means 'mourned loudly,' and 投水 means 'jumped into the water.' This phrase describes the dog's reaction.
  • 口衔裹具，与共浮沉 - 口衔 means 'with its mouth,' 裹具 means 'the wrapped package,' and 与共浮沉 means 'floated together.' This phrase describes the dog's loyalty.
  • 流荡不知几里，达浅搁乃止 - 流荡 means 'drifted,' 不知几里 means 'for several miles,' 达浅搁 means 'reached a shallow area,' and 乃止 means 'and stopped.' This phrase describes the dog's journey.
  • 犬泅出，至有人处 - 犬 means 'dog,' 泅出 means 'swam out,' and 至有人处 means 'to a place where people were.' This phrase describes the dog's rescue.
  • 狼信哀吠 - 狼信 means 'with great faith,' and 哀吠 means 'mournfully barked.' This phrase describes the dog's desperate call for help.
  • 或以为异，从之而往 - 或 means 'someone,' 以为异 means 'thought it strange,' and 从之而往 means 'followed it.' This phrase describes the people's response.
  • 见毡束水中，引出断其绳 - 见毡束水中 means 'saw the bundle in the water,' 引出 means 'pulled it out,' and 断其绳 means 'cut the rope.' This phrase describes the rescue.
  • 客固未死，始言其情 - 客 means 'the guest,' 固 means 'indeed,' 未死 means 'was not dead,' and 始言其情 means 'then told his story.' This phrase describes the merchant's survival and revelation.
  • 复哀舟人载还芜湖 - 复 means 'again,' 哀 means 'pitied,' 舟人 means 'boatman,' and 载还芜湖 means 'took him back to Wuhu.' This phrase describes the merchant's request for help.
  • 将以伺盗船之归 - 以 means 'to,' 伺 means 'wait for,' and 盗船之归 means 'the bandits' return.' This phrase describes the merchant's plan.
  • 登舟失犬，心甚悼焉 - 登舟 means 'boarded the boat,' 失犬 means 'lost the dog,' and 心甚悼焉 means 'was very saddened.' This phrase describes the merchant's emotional state.
  • 抵关三四日，估辑如林 - 抵关 means 'arrived at the checkpoint,' 三四日 means 'three or four days,' and 估辑如林 means 'there were many boats like a forest.' This phrase describes the scene at the checkpoint.
  • 而盗船不见 - 而 means 'but,' and 盗船不见 means 'the bandits' boat was not seen.' This phrase describes the absence of the bandits' boat.

[1251]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 適有同鄉估客將 - Phrase meaning 'happened to have a fellow townsman merchant who was carrying something.'
  • 忽犬自来，望客大嗥 - Phrase describing a sudden event where the dog came and barked loudly at the guest.
  • 唤之却走 - Phrase describing the action of calling the dog, which then turned away and ran.
  • 客下舟趁之 - Phrase describing the guest getting off the boat to pursue the dog.
  • 犬奔上一舟，啮人胫股，挞之不解 - Phrase describing the dog running onto a boat and biting a person, and beating it did not stop it.
  • 衣服与舟皆易，故不得而认之矣 - Phrase explaining why the dog could not be recognized.
  • 缚而搜之，则裹金犹在 - Phrase describing the action of tying the dog up and searching it, finding the wrapped gold still there.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 神女 - In Chinese literature, the term '神女' often refers to a beautiful and ethereal female figure, sometimes with supernatural qualities. It can also refer to the goddesses or deities associated with water and fertility.
  • 郡 - Refers to a prefecture or administrative division during the Han Dynasty.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '偶入郡，飲醉過市' describes a sequence of events: entering the prefecture, getting drunk, and passing through the market. The order and timing of these events are clear but need to be conveyed naturally in English.
  • The placement of '聞高門中有箫声' is important. It indicates that the sound of the flute was heard while passing through the market, adding a sensory detail to the narrative.
  • The clause '詢知為開壽筵者，然門庭殊清寂' provides additional information about the scene. It explains that the person inquired and found out it was a birthday banquet, but the courtyard and entrance were unusually quiet and desolate. The contrast between the expected celebration and the actual quietness is significant.

  • Sentences such as '以草菅人命罚作畜' and '稽某所为，曾挞其父母，其罪维均' must be read as single, continuous punishments and investigations, respectively.

  • Sentences such as '一日卧街头，适有客自南携金毛犬来' and '大如狸，心易其小，龁之' must be read as single, continuous encounters and sequences of actions, respectively.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 冥王 - Refers to the King of Hell, a figure in Chinese mythology.
  • 繁缨棨戟 - Elaborate decorations and insignia of high rank, often associated with military and administrative officials.

  • 城隍、郡司 - Local deities and officials in Chinese folklore, often serving as protectors and administrators of the local area.

  • 上下其鹰鸷之手 - Using their sharp and fierce hands to oppress, a metaphor for oppressive actions.
  • 罔念夫民贫 - Not caring about the poverty of the people, a criticism of neglecting the welfare of the common folk.
  • 飞扬其狙狯之奸 - Displaying cunning and deceit, a reference to manipulative and deceitful behavior.
  • 鬼瘦 - Making ghosts thin, a metaphor for causing suffering and hardship.

  • 是真吾弟之乱命也 - This is truly my brother's rash command, expressing disapproval or frustration.

  • 公子哀伤，如丧手足 - The young master mourned deeply, as if he had lost a limb, expressing profound grief.
  • 然窃疑其嘱异 - However, he secretly suspected that his instructions were unusual, indicating a sense of unease or doubt.
  • 俟素秋他出，启而视之 - After Suqiu left, he opened it to see, suggesting a moment of curiosity or concern.

  • 兄弟何所隔阂 - What is there to separate brothers? expressing a sense of unity or reconciliation.
  • 所以然者非避兄也 - The reason for this is not to avoid my brother, indicating a rationalization or explanation.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 父既归，坚辞欲去 - The father has returned home, and [the son] firmly declines to leave. This is a polite and formal way of expressing refusal.
  • 兄弟不忍 - The brothers cannot bear [the situation]. This implies a strong emotional reaction.
  • 析产而三之 - To divide the property into three parts. This is a formal expression used in legal or familial contexts.
  • 子得二，女得一也 - The sons receive two parts, and the daughter receives one part. This is a specific distribution of inheritance.
  • 大娘固辞 - The eldest sister firmly declines. This is another polite and formal way of expressing refusal.
  • 吾等非姊，乌有今日 - Without our elder sister, we would not be where we are today. This is a rhetorical statement emphasizing gratitude.
  • 福禄闻之皆流涕 - Hearing this, they all wept. This indicates a strong emotional response.
  • 使工人治其第，皆与己等 - They had workers build their homes, all equal to their own. This suggests a generous and equal treatment.
  • 祸之而益福之 - They cursed him but instead brought him more blessings. This is a paradoxical statement indicating a positive outcome despite initial negative intentions.
  • 深自愧悔 - They deeply regretted and felt ashamed. This expresses a strong sense of remorse.
  • 又仰其富，思交欢之 - They also admired his wealth and wanted to befriend him. This indicates a desire for social connection.
  • 因以贺仲阶进，备物而往 - So they sent a gift through their middleman, well-prepared. This shows a formal and thoughtful gesture.
  • 却之不得，系羊庭树 - He could not refuse, so he tied the sheep to a tree in the courtyard. This indicates a polite but firm acceptance.
  • 夜有僮被仆殴，忿趋树下，解羊索自经死 - At night, a servant was beaten by another servant, became angry, rushed to the tree, untied the sheep, and hanged himself. This describes a tragic event.
  • 其福之不如其祸之也 - His blessings were less than his curses. This is a paradoxical statement indicating a negative outcome despite initial positive intentions.
  • 后魏老，贫而作丐，仇每周以布粟而德报之 - Later, Wei became old, poor, and a beggar, and his enemies provided him with cloth and grain, which he gratefully accepted. This shows a change in circumstances and a final act of kindness.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 父既归 - The father returning home. This is a common scenario in traditional Chinese family life.
  • 兄弟不忍 - The brothers' emotional response. This reflects the importance of family ties and emotional bonds.
  • 析产而三之 - Dividing property. This is a traditional practice in Chinese inheritance laws.
  • 子得二，女得一也 - Gender-based distribution of inheritance. This reflects historical gender norms in Chinese society.
  • 大娘固辞 - The eldest sister's refusal. This shows respect for the eldest sibling's role in the family.
  • 吾等非姊，乌有今日 - Gratitude to the eldest sister. This emphasizes the importance of family support.
  • 福禄闻之皆流涕 - Emotional response to a story. This reflects the cultural value placed on storytelling and emotional connection.
  • 使工人治其第，皆与己等 - Building homes. This shows a commitment to equality and generosity.
  • 祸之而益福之 - Paradoxical outcome. This reflects the belief in karma and the unexpected consequences of actions.
  • 深自愧悔 - Regret and shame. This shows a deep sense of moral responsibility.
  • 又仰其富，思交欢之 - Admiration for wealth. This reflects the social value placed on material success.
  • 因以贺仲阶进，备物而往 - Sending gifts. This shows a formal and thoughtful gesture.
  • 却之不得，系羊庭树 - Accepting a gift. This shows a polite and firm acceptance.
  • 夜有僮被仆殴，忿趋树下，解羊索自经死 - Tragic event. This reflects the harsh realities of life and the consequences of violence.
  • 其福之不如其祸之也 - Negative outcome. This reflects the belief in the balance of good and bad fortune.
  • 后魏老，贫而作丐，仇每周以布粟而德报之 - Change in circumstances. This shows the cyclical nature of life and the importance of kindness.

[1448]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 忽于深树内觌面遇女郎 - Literal description meaning 'suddenly encountered a young lady face to face in the deep woods.'
  • 幸无他人 - Means 'fortunately no one else was around.'
  • 大喜投地 - Means 'greatly delighted and fell to the ground,' expressing extreme joy and possibly shock.
  • 女郎近曳之 - Means 'the young lady drew him close.'
  • 忽闻异香竟体 - Means 'suddenly smelled an unusual fragrance all over,' describing a sudden and pervasive scent.
  • 即以手握玉腕而起 - Means 'immediately took her hand and helped her up,' indicating a gentle and respectful action.
  • 指肤软腻，使人骨节欲酥 - Means 'her fingers were soft and smooth, making one's bones and joints feel almost melting,' describing a tactile sensation.
  • 正欲有言，老妪忽至 - Means 'just as he was about to speak, an old woman suddenly arrived.'
  • 女令隐身石后 - Means 'the young lady instructed him to hide behind a stone.'
  • 夜以花梯度墙 - Means 'at night, use a ladder made of flowers to climb over the wall.'
  • 四面红窗者即妾居也 - Means 'the place with red windows on all sides is where I live.'
  • 生怅然，魂魄飞散，莫知所往 - Means 'he was filled with melancholy, his soul and spirit scattered, not knowing where to go.'
  • 至夜移梯登南垣 - Means 'by night, move the ladder and climb the southern wall.'
  • 果有红窗 - Means 'indeed, there was a red window.'
  • 室中闻敲棋声 - Means 'he heard the sound of chess pieces being knocked.'
  • 伫立不敢复前 - Means 'he stood still, too afraid to go further.'
  • 姑逾垣归 - Means 'temporarily climbed over the wall and returned.'
  • 凡三往复，漏已三催 - Means 'after three trips back and forth, the night clock had struck three times,' indicating the passage of time.
  • 生伏梯上，闻妪出云 - Means 'he crouched on the ladder and heard the old woman say.'
  • 梯也，谁置此？ - Means 'it's a ladder, who put it here?'
  • 呼婢共移去之 - Means 'called the maid to move it away.'
  • 生登垣，欲下无阶，恨悒而返 - Means 'he climbed the wall again, wanting to come down but having no steps, and returned feeling disappointed and sad.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 玉腕 - Refers to a beautiful and delicate wrist, often used to describe the hands of a lady.
  • 老妪 - Refers to an old woman, often a wise or authoritative figure.
  • 花梯 - A ladder made of flowers, often a metaphor for something delicate and ephemeral.

  • 棋 - Refers to chess, a game of strategy and intellect.
  • 漏 - Refers to a night clock, often used to measure the passage of time.
  • 恨悒 - Refers to a feeling of disappointment and sadness.

[1451]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 去后，衾枕皆染异香 - Metaphor for the lingering influence or memory of someone.
  • 三两夜辄一至 - Recurring, almost ritualistic visit.
  • 生惑之不复思归 - State of enchantment or obsession.
  • 囊橐既空欲货马 - Financial desperation, selling one's horse.
  • 君以妾故，泻囊质衣，情所不忍 - Reluctance to burden the man further.
  • 又去代步，千余里将何以归 - Practical difficulty of returning over a thousand miles.
  • 妾有私蓄，卿可助装 - Willingness to help with expenses.
  • 感卿情好，抚臆誓肌，不足论报 - Deep gratitude and a sense of inadequacy in repayment.
  • 而又贪鄙以耗卿财，何以为人乎 - Moral dilemma of being greedy and wasteful of the man's money.
  • 女固强之 - Insistence on lending the money.
  • 姑假君 - Generosity in lending the money.
  • 遂捉生臂至一桑树下 - Physical action of grabbing the man's arm and leading him to a mulberry tree.
  • 指一石曰：“转之！” - Command to turn the stone.
  • 又拔头上簪，刺土数十下 - Ritualistic action of pulling out a hairpin and sticking it into the ground.
  • 又曰：“爬之。” - Command to dig.
  • 瓮口已见 - Result of the digging, the mouth of the jar was visible.
  • 女探入，出白镪近五十余两 - Amount of money, nearly fifty taels of silver.
  • 生把臂止之，不听，又出数十铤，生强分其半而后掩之 - Negotiation and final action of taking half of the ingots.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 衾枕 - Symbols of comfort and intimacy.
  • 异香 - Strange or unusual fragrance, often associated with supernatural or magical elements.
  • 囊橐 - Purse or bag, a common term for personal belongings.
  • 货马 - Selling one's horse, a significant act of desperation in a time when horses were essential for travel.
  • 私蓄 - Personal savings, indicating the woman's financial independence.
  • 白镪 - Silver, a valuable commodity in ancient China.
  • 铤 - Ingots, a form of silver currency.
